name: Pakhet
description: Pakhet, also known as Pakht, Pachet, or Pasht, is an ancient Egyptian lioness goddess of war, hunting, and the desert. Her name translates to 'She Who Tears', reflecting her fierce nature and predatory skills. She was primarily worshipped in Middle Egypt, particularly at Beni Hasan, where her temple, Speos Artemidos, was carved into the cliffs. Pakhet was often associated with protecting the pharaoh and ensuring successful hunts. She was seen as a fierce protector, capable of destroying enemies and defending the innocent. Her connection to the desert emphasized her wild, untamed power. Pakhet was also linked to the sun and the moon, embodying both solar and lunar aspects. She was sometimes considered a local form of Sekhmet or Bastet, sharing similar feline attributes and protective roles, but maintaining her unique identity and characteristics. Her worship involved rituals and offerings to appease her power and secure her favor, particularly in matters of protection and success in hunting. She was seen as a powerful force to be reckoned with, commanding respect and invoking fear in those who dared to challenge her. Pakhet's dual nature as both a fierce warrior and a protective mother made her a complex and revered figure in the Egyptian pantheon.
subtitle: The Lioness Who Tears

keyMyths.title: Protection of the Pharaoh
keyMyths.description: Pakhet was believed to protect the pharaoh in battle, ensuring his victory and safety. Her fierce nature made her a formidable guardian against enemies of the state.

keyMyths.title: The Huntress
keyMyths.description: Pakhet's association with hunting made her a patron of hunters, ensuring successful expeditions and plentiful game. Offerings were made to her to secure a fruitful hunt.

keyMyths.title: Lunar Association
keyMyths.description: Pakhet's connection to the moon linked her to the cycles of nature and the mysteries of the night. She was seen as a powerful lunar deity alongside her solar aspects.

sources.author: Richard H. Wilkinson
sources.work: The Complete Gods and Goddesses of Ancient Egypt

sources.author: Emily Teeter
sources.work: Religion and Ritual in Ancient Egypt
sources.description: Exploration of religious practices and beliefs.
sources.text: Exploration of religious practices and beliefs.
